sglang.title: env: add knob to control SWA eviction interval
sglang.author: happierpig
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
SWA eviction only runs every sliding_window_size decode steps per request. Between evictions, each request holds up to 2 * sliding_window_size SWA tokens — double what the sliding window needs.
Adding a knob to trade-off between the token waste and per-forward overhead.

Modifications

Ac

sglang.title: ci: skip full rerun when sgl-kernel wheel already built
sglang.author: jasperjiaguo

Summary
When /rerun-failed-ci is used on a PR with sgl-kernel changes, the handler currently does a full rerun() to ensure sgl-kernel-build-wheels runs. But if the wheel already built successfully for the current commit, the full rerun is unnecessary — it retriggers all tests including flaky ones (e

sglang.title: [Anthropic] Fix clock mismatch in received_time causing negative Prometheus metrics
sglang.author: lixuwei2333
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fixes #22249
The Anthropic API entrypoint (serving.py) uses time.time() (wall clock, epoch-based) for received_time, while downstream metrics computation in req_time_stats.py calculates deltas against time.perf_counter() (monotonic). This clock mismatch produces large negative values  in

sglang.title: [fix] Fix deadlock and TOCTOU race in pause-aware weight update locking
sglang.author: maocheng23
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Fix writer lock deadlock in update_weights_from_ipc and post_process_weights during pause_generation mode
Fix TOCTOU (time-of-check to time-of-use) race in all four weight update paths: update_weights_from_distributed, update_weights_from_tensor, update_weights_from_ipc, post_process_weight

Minimal repro shape:
python3 -m sglang.launch_server \
  --model-path /path/to/Qwen3-32B \
  --trust-remote-code \
  --json-mod

For DP attention with Expert Parallelism (EP), the default MoE communication path performs two separate operations after the MoE kernel:

tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce — reduces expert outputs across all DP workers
dp_scatter — extracts each worker's local token slice from the global r

sglang.author: yctseng0211

Motivation

Remove push trigger to avoid redundant CI runs on every merge to main, reducing AMD runner pressure
Add schedule trigger (every 6 hours) to periodically validate main branch
For scheduled runs, all stages (A/B/C) run in parallel by introducing wait-for-stage-a-amd and wait-for-stage-b-am

sglang.title: [CI] Reinstall flashinfer-jit-cache on CUDA version mismatch
sglang.author: Fridge003
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Extract the CUDA version suffix (e.g., cu130) from the installed flashinfer-jit-cache package version (e.g., 0.6.7+cu130)
Compare it against the runner's CU_VERSION; if they differ, force reinstall even when the base version matches
Prevents using a jit cache built for the wrong CUDA versio

Fixes #37697
What's the problem
When using /v1/responses with previous_response_id, the instructions from the prior response carry over into the new response. Per the OpenAI spec, instructions should NOT carry over:

"When using along with previous_response_id, the instructions from a previous respo

config.json (or configs loaded programmatically) will have hf_config.architectures = None.
The existing code used getatt

A previous refactor here correctly sliced decode_metadata.seq_lens to batch_size for fp8_paged_mqa_logits, but missed two downstream consumers t

Purpose
(Generated with Claude)
This PR scopes the MXFP4 quantization implementation to GPT-OSS checkpoints
while keeping generic MXFP4 support for other models (e.g. Quark).
Changes
quantization/mxfp4.py (renamed from gpt_oss_mxfp4.py):

Introduces Mxfp4Config as the canonical base class (get_name(

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Use is_integrated to detect UMA GPUs for memory reporting
vllm.author: haosdent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #35313.
On UMA (Unified Memory Architecture) systems like GH200, DGX Spark, and Jetson Orin, cudaMemGetInfo doesn't account for reclaimable OS memory (page cache, buffers), causing vLLM to underestimate free GPU memory and fail at startup with:
ValueError: Free memory on device cuda:0

vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Small UX improvement to allow storing performance results in json, which is also more aligned with the rest of the bench suite (rather than just excel out)

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Kernel][ROCm] Fix triton_w4a16 scales mismatch when BLOCK_K > group_size
vllm.author: JartX
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The Triton W4A16 GEMM kernel (triton_w4a16_gemm_kernel) loads quantization
scales and zero points from a single group index per BLOCK_K tile, but the
kernel launcher does not enforce BLOCK_K <= group_size. When BLOCK_K
exceeds group_size, a single tile spans multiple scale groups, but only the
first

vllm.title: [Doc] Fix Python-only build 404 fallback guidance

Add a reliab

vllm.title: [Core][Metrics] expose waiting request breakdown via labeled metric (capacity/deferred)
vllm.author: mukesh-hai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The existing vllm:num_requests_waiting gauge combines two meaningfully different request populations into a single number, making it impossible to distinguish load-driven backlog from constraint-driven blocking when debugging scheduler behavior under production load.
This PR introduces vllm:

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Pooling] Fix silent weight corruption with buffer-reusing iterators
vllm.author: pedramr
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
ModelForPooling.load_weights uses (*seen_weights, *weights) which eagerly
consumes the entire iterator at generator creation time. With runai_streamer
(RUNAI_STREAMER_MEMORY_LIMIT=0), the shared I/O buffer is overwritten before
tensors are consumed, silently corrupting all pooling/embedding

vllm.title: Bug/test eagle dp v0
vllm.author: Monishver11
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Flaky Tests in EAGLE DP
Issue: #31913
This PR resolves the flaky EAGLE DP test in the continuous integration (CI) environment.
For additional context, related issues and fixes can be found here: pytorch/pytorch#170563.

Root Cause: Incomplete batch invariance coverage on L4 GPUs under torch.compile.

Refactor DelegatingParser.parse_delta(). The original code used a chain of if/elif/elif/else with the "both parsers" case inlined as a large block. The new code extracts two helper methods (_in_reasoning_phase, _in_tool_call_phase) and restructures the logic into sequential if blocks, which

Evals:
VoxPopuli and AMI_ASR evals on par b

vllm.author: TheEpicDolphin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
FULL cudagraphs are currently only used for the position 1+ drafting phase. In this PR, I apply FULL cudagraphs to the Eagle prefill path as well to reduce the CPU dispatch overhead in EagleSpeculator.propose.
Benchmarks
H200
I ran an exhaustive set of accuracy and performance benchmarks acr

vllm.title: [LMCache][MP] optimize save when mla enabled
vllm.author: chunxiaozheng
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When MLA is enabled, store or retrieve requests only need to be sent once in multi workers, which can greatly reduce the number of requests in the server.
The current PR only modifies store requests and will modify retrieve requests in the next request.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix Gemma4 tool parser converting bare `null` to string `"null"`
vllm.author: KimuGenie
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When serving a Gemma4 model with --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser gemma4, a tool parameter that accepts null (e.g. {"type": "string", "nullable": true}) is mis-serialized when the model emits the token sequence bare — i.e. param:null — inside a <|tool_call>call:name{...}<tool_cal

vllm.title: Fix Responses API streaming for multiple auto tool calls
vllm.author: noobHappylife
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR addresses:

In responses api with tool_choice="auto" for multiple tool calls, currently the funcation_call events will merge the multiple tool calls into 1 function_call_arguments.

After the PR:

tool_choice="auto" handles multiple tool calls properly, with independent function_call
